description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a predominantly brownish-gray coloration. The surface is rough and uneven, featuring natural fractures, crevices, and weathered patches that suggest exposure to the elements. One side of the rock displays a visible marking or writing, possibly an identification code or catalog number, indicating prior human handling or study. The coloration varies from dark brown to lighter tan and gray, with subtle mineral streaks and patches of discoloration. The rock appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings, and its mass and size would make it a substantial obstacle in an urban environment. Its natural, unpolished appearance suggests it could be used as a landscape feature, barrier, or decorative element in parks or public spaces.
description: A rugged, brownish-gray rock with an irregular, angular shape and rough, weathered surface, featuring visible markings on one side.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the rock, highlighting its angular edges and a mix of brown and gray tones.
description_view_1: The narrow end of the rock is visible here, with a darker, more uniform surface and subtle vertical striations.
description_view_2: This side reveals a lighter, weathered surface with a clear human-made marking or code written in black.
description_view_3: The rock's top and side are visible, showing a mix of rough textures and color variations, with no visible markings.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
